subject: Computer Networking
title: Wi-Fi Offloading
description: A micro-lesson that makes Wi-Fi Offloading usable.

hook.text: Your phone switches to Wi-Fi at home, saving your mobile data. Carriers encourage this — it saves them bandwidth too.

buildup.text: Wi-Fi offloading moves traffic from the cellular network to local Wi-Fi. Both the user and carrier benefit.

discovery.text: Passpoint lets your phone automatically join trusted Wi-Fi networks without you typing a password.

twist.text: Over half of all mobile data is actually carried by Wi-Fi. The cellular network couldn't handle it all alone.

climax.text: 5G small cells and Wi-Fi 6 are converging. Future networks may blend both seamlessly without users noticing.

punchline.text: Wi-Fi offloading keeps cellular networks from collapsing.

quiz.question: Why do carriers encourage Wi-Fi offloading?
options: It reduces load on their cellular network
Wi-Fi is always faster than cellular
It allows them to charge more for data
